feed.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions

entry.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions
entry.summary:
The email outlines a structured approach to organizing knowledge within a specific domain, likely related to blockchain governance and documentation. The hierarchy begins with the "Constitutional Tier," which includes various levels of foundational documents such as minimal documents, whitepapers, and genesis blocks. These form the base level of the knowledge structure.
Following this, the "Drafting Record" tier is described, comprising original communications and writings from key figures in the field, like Satoshi Nakamoto's emails and forum posts. This tier captures the initial intentions and discussions surrounding the creation and development of blockchain technologies.
The next level up is the "Interpretative Scholarship," which consists of high-quality theoretical or practical documents, plans, and concepts. This tier aims to provide deep insights and thoughtful interpretations of the underlying principles and operational mechanisms of blockchain technologies.
Finally, the "Precedent" tier includes Bitcoin Improvement Proposals (BIPs) and similar documents that set precedents within the blockchain community. These documents are crucial for understanding how rules and practices have evolved over time.
The sender also notes that their project is a work in progress and not yet available as open source, but they express an interest in collaborating and possibly converging ideas in the future. This openness to collaboration suggests a willingness to integrate and refine the proposed structure with additional insights from others in the field.
